component.name: Skrizhal
component.summary: Build a reusable YAML database of CV elements

description.p: Skrizhal is a GTK4 editor for CV/résumé elements — jobs, education, awards, publications, service, and more — each stored as a YAML entry with a citation-style key. Instead of hand-typing (and hand-toggling) a new CV for every application, build the database once and let a style layer pull entries in as needed.

ul.li: Sidebar with search and type/tag filtering, sorted most-recent-first
Structured form for common fields (key, type, title, organization, location, date, tags, description), with a suggestion popover for known entry types
Dynamic "Additional Fields" editor for type-specific data (degree, DOI, award amount, and anything else a given entry type needs)
Raw YAML toggle — edit an entry's serialized block directly when the structured form doesn't fit
Add, duplicate, and delete entries, with a confirmation before delete
Manage Tags dialog — rename a tag everywhere at once, or merge two tags by renaming one onto the other
Configurable data file location, with a guard against saving over a file that failed to parse

description.p: Designed to work alongside Zerkalo, which will be able to pull entries into a Typst CV document in a future release.

screenshot.caption: Skrizhal editing a CV entry, with type-specific fields and tag filtering

description.p: 0.3.1 "Clear Ledger" — Undo/redo (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z) across every mutation. New File, Open, Save As, and Preferences for managing the data file location, replacing the old open-existing-only "Choose Data File…" picker. Default data file location moved to ~/Documents/Zerkalo/cv-elements.yaml. Files with a malformed entry now load every other entry instead of failing entirely, and old files using the pre-rename `type:` field load correctly. The spreadsheet bulk-edit grid, introduced in 0.3.0, has been removed — it never replaced what it was meant to and its add-row flow could freeze the app; entries are now added and edited one at a time through the sidebar and detail pane.

0.3.0 "Full Ledger" — Auto-generated entry keys with live duplicate feedback, categories replacing the old `type` field, a Date Type dropdown (Single/Range/Ongoing) with separate Start/End fields, a first-run Field Guide, and a spreadsheet bulk-edit view (removed in 0.3.1).

0.2.0 "First Etching" — First packaged release. GTK4/libadwaita editor: sidebar with search/type/tag filtering, structured entry form with a dynamic type-specific fields editor, raw YAML toggle, add/duplicate/delete with delete confirmation, Manage Tags dialog with rename/merge, and a configurable data file location.
